title: `skopeo copy` to `oci-archive:` does not copy over platform and annotations when `--all` is not used
body:
Assume there is an image in a registry with annotations and platform information:
```
$ skopeo inspect --raw docker://docker.io/library/busybox | jq | head -25
{
  "manifests": [
    {
      "annotations": {
        "com.docker.official-images.bashbrew.arch": "amd64",
        "org.opencontainers.image.base.name": "scratch",
        "org.opencontainers.image.created": "2025-10-21T23:04:45Z",
        "org.opencontainers.image.revision": "3c91bcd92b2cd6c113a87c39bcada57fbccf00b0",
        "org.opencontainers.image.source": "https://github.com/docker-library/busybox.git",
        "org.opencontainers.image.url": "https://hub.docker.com/_/busybox",
        "org.opencontainers.image.version": "1.37.0-glibc"
      },
      "digest": "sha256:870e815c3a50dd0f6b40efddb319c72c32c3ee340b5a3e8945904232ccd12f44",
      "mediaType": "application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json",
      "platform": {
        "architecture": "amd64",
        "os": "linux"
      },
      "size": 610
    },
    {
      "annotations": {
        "com.docker.official-images.bashbrew.arch": "amd64",
        "vnd.docker.reference.digest": "sha256:870e815c3a50dd0f6b40efddb319c72c32c3ee340b5a3e8945904232ccd12f44",
        "vnd.docker.reference.type": "attestation-manifest"
```

We'd like to be able to have that image locally:
```
$ skopeo copy docker://docker.io/library/busybox oci-archive:/tmp/busybox.tar
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob e59838ecfec5 done   | 
Copying config 08ef35a1c3 done   | 
Writing manifest to image destination
$ tar tvf /tmp/busybox.tar
drwxr-xr-x 0/0               0 2026-01-08 14:21 blobs/
drwxr-xr-x 0/0               0 2026-01-08 14:21 blobs/sha256/
-rw-r--r-- 0/0             459 2026-01-08 14:21 blobs/sha256/08ef35a1c3f050afbbd64194ffd1b8d5878659f5491567f26d1c814513ae9649
-rw-r--r-- 0/0             610 2026-01-08 14:21 blobs/sha256/870e815c3a50dd0f6b40efddb319c72c32c3ee340b5a3e8945904232ccd12f44
-rw-r--r-- 0/0         2213754 2026-01-08 14:21 blobs/sha256/e59838ecfec5e79eb4371e9995ef86c8000fe1c67d7b9fa7b57e996d9ba772ff
-rw-r--r-- 0/0             186 2026-01-08 14:21 index.json
-rw-r--r-- 0/0              30 2026-01-08 14:21 oci-layout
```

However, the `index.json` looks nothing like the wealth of information provided by the registry, there's just the manifest digest present:
```
$ tar x -O -f /tmp/busybox.tar index.json | jq
{
  "schemaVersion": 2,
  "manifests": [
    {
      "mediaType": "application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json",
      "digest": "sha256:870e815c3a50dd0f6b40efddb319c72c32c3ee340b5a3e8945904232ccd12f44",
      "size": 610
    }
  ]
}
```

It is possible to get the information with `--all` which seems to get nested image JSONs:
```
$ skopeo copy --all docker://docker.io/library/busybox oci-archive:/tmp/busybox.tar
Getting image list signatures
Copying 17 images generated from 17 images in list
Copying image sha256:870e815c3a50dd0f6b40efddb319c72c32c3ee340b5a3e8945904232ccd12f44 (1/17)
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob e59838ecfec5 done   | 
Copying config 08ef35a1c3 done   | 
Writing manifest to image destination
Copying image sha256:b42860cea369d523da98883e26b67260257f25fa1efa0e3f6336109b37cf3049 (2/17)
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 0f4360cf3c3e done   | 
Copying config 233cb43a47 done   | 
Writing manifest to image destination
Copying image sha256:44b2c602cb838fd6a647d6d48cf0e6f56227351b2903263d60637ebd26545a8d (3/17)
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob f1d8893ddf81 done   | 
[...]
$ tar x -O -f /tmp/busybox.tar index.json | jq
{
  "schemaVersion": 2,
  "manifests": [
    {
      "mediaType": "application/vnd.oci.image.index.v1+json",
      "digest": "sha256:2383baad1860bbe9d8a7a843775048fd07d8afe292b94bd876df64a69aae7cb1",
      "size": 9535
    }
  ]
}
$ tar x -O -f /tmp/busybox.tar blobs/sha256/2383baad1860bbe9d8a7a843775048fd07d8afe292b94bd876df64a69aae7cb1 | jq | head -25
{
  "manifests": [
    {
      "annotations": {
        "com.docker.official-images.bashbrew.arch": "amd64",
        "org.opencontainers.image.base.name": "scratch",
        "org.opencontainers.image.created": "2025-10-21T23:04:45Z",
        "org.opencontainers.image.revision": "3c91bcd92b2cd6c113a87c39bcada57fbccf00b0",
        "org.opencontainers.image.source": "https://github.com/docker-library/busybox.git",
        "org.opencontainers.image.url": "https://hub.docker.com/_/busybox",
        "org.opencontainers.image.version": "1.37.0-glibc"
      },
      "digest": "sha256:870e815c3a50dd0f6b40efddb319c72c32c3ee340b5a3e8945904232ccd12f44",
      "mediaType": "application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json",
      "platform": {
        "architecture": "amd64",
        "os": "linux"
      },
      "size": 610
    },
    {
      "annotations": {
        "com.docker.official-images.bashbrew.arch": "amd64",
        "vnd.docker.reference.digest": "sha256:870e815c3a50dd0f6b40efddb319c72c32c3ee340b5a3e8945904232ccd12f44",
        "vnd.docker.reference.type": "attestation-manifest"
```

However, it is quite wasteful to fetch everything when just the metadata is needed.

comments.body:
Thanks for your report.

Yes, that’s how that works now. The top-level index in `oci-archive` is similar to a "on-registry list of images in this repo”, it is _not_ a part of any single image. So, if you copy a per-architecture image, that is “rooted” in the per-architecture manifest.json, and annotations on the index are a not a part of the per-architecture image. (Compare when copying that per-architecture image to a registry: there is no location where those annotations could be stored either.)

If this information _must_ be maintained along with each per-architecture image, I think it belongs in the per-architecture manifest, and not in the index.

Thanks for the analysis.

I'm not sure I understand how the "on-registry list of images in this repo" semantics of the single-architecture `oci-archive` as a result of `skopeo copy` differs from the "true registry output of `skopeo inspect --raw docker://docker.io/library/busybox`". Both are of type `application/vnd.oci.image.index.v1+json` (well, the skopeo output will be marked as such when https://github.com/containers/container-libs/issues/573 is addressed) and both are a collection of manifests (`application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json`).

Since the single-architecture archive only has one manifest, it is implied that all those manifest (that one :-) ) belong to a single image.

But note that the `annotations` and `platform` that I look at here are in the `manifests` entries, not some top-level image `annotations` which at least in case of `docker://docker.io/library/busybox` (or direct curl from `https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/library/busybox/manifests/latest`) seem not present.

Fetching for example `https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/library/busybox/manifests/sha256:870e815c3a50dd0f6b40efddb319c72c32c3ee340b5a3e8945904232ccd12f44` shows that there are fewer annotations in that manifest JSON than in the top-level index for the given manifest (but there are some). OTOH, `platform` is missing completely, most probably because https://github.com/opencontainers/image-spec/blob/main/manifest.md does not list `platform` as expected property at all.

So the way the OCI specification lays things out means that if we start at the per-architecture manifest level, we lose quite significant amount of information because `platform` is not stored there -- it is only available "from outside". Annotations can be stored both on the image level, in manifest entries in the image JSON, and in the manifest itself.

If you suggest that the specification should have allowed for the `platform` to be inside of the manifest JSON as well, and the annotations to be repeated both in the manifest JSON and in the manifest entries of the image, I'd agree. But the reality is that's not how the current specs and build and registry tools do it, and I'm mostly pointing out that skopeo (as a very useful tool) seems to miss copying some information that it could copy without conflicting with the specs, making things easier for consumer of the archives.

> I'm not sure I understand how the "on-registry list of images in this repo" semantics of the single-architecture `oci-archive` as a result of `skopeo copy` differs from the "true registry output of `skopeo inspect --raw docker://docker.io/library/busybox`".

The reproducer is
```sh
skopeo copy docker://docker.io/library/busybox oci-archive:/tmp/busybox.tar
```

An on-registry equivalent,
```sh
skopeo copy [no --all] docker://docker.io/library/busybox:latest docker://registry.example/busybox-our-single-arch:experiment
```
is _equivalently_ going to discard the annotations and properties in the index, because there _is_ no index at the destination to put them in. [1]

If you will, `oci-archive:$path` is an equivalent to `docker://$registry/$namespace:ALL_TAGS_SIMULTANEOUSLY`, not an equivalent to `docker://$registry/$namespace:latest`.

That is stretching the analogy a bit, but the point is that the top-level `index.json` in `oci-archive`, as currently designed, is _not_ a part of the image. That’s also visible in how a `copy --all` to `oci-archive:` works: the original image’s index is stored as a nested index within the top-level `index.json`, it is not written directly to the top-level `index.json` file.

---

> So the way the OCI specification lays things out means that if we start at the per-architecture manifest level, we lose quite significant amount of information

Yes.

I think [1] demonstrates that this is a _general_ problem for that choice of annotations / properties: if you want to work with individual per-platform images, the data simply should not be stored in indices.

Now, to the argument that `skopeo copy` could take the annotations and other data from the index (outside of “the manifest that is being copied” and write them into the `oci-archive:`: Yes, that could could be written, but that would be a special case, sort of breaking the “transport” abstraction. That’s not a show-stopper…

… but think about integrity / signatures: any such data outside of the manifest is not authenticated by digest references or signatures (because they also root the integrity at the per-arch manifest). And it would need special handling for transporting across registries (and I always recommend using registries over tar archives).

So far it doesn’t look very attractive to me to build such a special case.

---

There’s https://github.com/containers/skopeo/issues/2463 tracking a RFE to have (something like?) `oci-archive:` where the whole archive is truly a single image, i.e. the `index.json` is a part of the image.

That’s sort of relevant here, but it would currently still be necessary to copy all architectures, so not truly what you are looking for.

Right now, there is `skopeo copy --multi-arch=index-only` (and, in Go, a feature to copy the index and only a selected subset of images, although that would require figuring out the right architecture manually). So it should be possible to do
```sh
skopeo copy docker://docker.io/busybox oci:busybox:image
skopeo copy --multi-arch=index-only --insecure-policy docker://docker.io/busybox oci:busybox:index
tar cf busybox.tar busybox
```

(this must use `oci:`, not `oci-archive:`, to add multiple images in separate steps). That would _record_ all the relevant annotations in the archive, but it would also require consumer specifically built for such a structure.)

And https://github.com/containers/skopeo/pull/1987 exists to allow copying the index and _a selected subset_ of per-platform images. I think that would be very close to what you want to happen (but the copied index would still be nested in a top-level `index.json`, without #2463 happening as well).

Thank you!

Feel free to close this issue if you feel the use-case is outside of the stated goals of skopeo.
I think there’s no harm in leaving it open for a few days (realistically: until the stale bot pings), in the hope that others might chime in with innovative ideas.

For the record, in https://github.com/freeipa/freeipa-container/commit/5f19b8d6567b62505f6ea390d382f04113b76b7d I now added putting the `org.opencontainers.image.*` labels to annotations as well, both to the image's manifest entries and to the manifest JSONs themselves.

The examples of annotations are
```
$ tar xz -O -f freeipa-server-fedora-43-x86_64/freeipa-server-fedora-43-x86_64.tar.gz index.json | jq '.manifests[].annotations'
{
  "io.containerd.image.name": "localhost/freeipa-server:fedora-43",
  "org.opencontainers.image.ref.name": "fedora-43",
  "org.opencontainers.image.created": "2026-01-09T08:31:12.025076053Z",
  "org.opencontainers.image.authors": "FreeIPA Developers <freeipa-devel@lists.fedorahosted.org>",
  "org.opencontainers.image.url": "https://github.com/freeipa/freeipa-container/actions/runs/20845928474",
  "org.opencontainers.image.documentation": "https://github.com/freeipa/freeipa-container",
  "org.opencontainers.image.source": "https://github.com/freeipa/freeipa-container/blob/5f19b8d6567b62505f6ea390d382f04113b76b7d/Dockerfile.fedora-43",
  "org.opencontainers.image.version": "4.13.0-rpms-7e62b52bff2aef0ce33396ee4e4d559a586de8e4ea0a29e053908c57e5101b44-gittree-2e973b157cc237934105744b928fb3b931a97242",
  "org.opencontainers.image.revision": "5f19b8d6567b62505f6ea390d382f04113b76b7d",
  "org.opencontainers.image.licenses": "Apache-2.0",
  "org.opencontainers.image.title": "FreeIPA server",
  "org.opencontainers.image.base.digest": "sha256:3a17720ba4dadff3500b9d614f5d35e2bd54f5f00e2d822b5467cb4cabb40a25",
  "org.opencontainers.image.base.name": "registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:43"
}
```
```
$ skopeo inspect --raw oci-archive:freeipa-server-fedora-43-x86_64/freeipa-server-fedora-43-x86_64.tar.gz | jq .annotations
{
  "org.opencontainers.image.created": "2026-01-09T08:31:12.025076053Z",
  "org.opencontainers.image.authors": "FreeIPA Developers <freeipa-devel@lists.fedorahosted.org>",
  "org.opencontainers.image.url": "https://github.com/freeipa/freeipa-container/actions/runs/20845928474",
  "org.opencontainers.image.documentation": "https://github.com/freeipa/freeipa-container",
  "org.opencontainers.image.source": "https://github.com/freeipa/freeipa-container/blob/5f19b8d6567b62505f6ea390d382f04113b76b7d/Dockerfile.fedora-43",
  "org.opencontainers.image.version": "4.13.0-rpms-7e62b52bff2aef0ce33396ee4e4d559a586de8e4ea0a29e053908c57e5101b44-gittree-2e973b157cc237934105744b928fb3b931a97242",
  "org.opencontainers.image.revision": "5f19b8d6567b62505f6ea390d382f04113b76b7d",
  "org.opencontainers.image.licenses": "Apache-2.0",
  "org.opencontainers.image.title": "FreeIPA server",
  "org.opencontainers.image.base.digest": "sha256:3a17720ba4dadff3500b9d614f5d35e2bd54f5f00e2d822b5467cb4cabb40a25",
  "org.opencontainers.image.base.name": "registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:43"
}
```
so in the image's manifest we have the `io.containerd.image.name` and `org.opencontainers.image.ref.name` extra values.

To get the annotations to the registry, we also had to change the `docker-archive:` to `oci-archive:` (via https://github.com/freeipa/freeipa-container/commit/798430a7bb4c505ef35833ab8b002d213eaa6543) when pushing the images to registry, either directly with `skopeo copy`, or when using `podman manifest create` and `push`.

The difference between the old way of doing it and the new one can be seen with
```
$ curl -s -H 'Accept: application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json' https://quay.io/v2/freeipa/freeipa-server/manifests/fedora-43-4.12.5 | jq | head -5
{
  "tag": "fedora-43-4.12.5",
  "name": "freeipa/freeipa-server",
  "architecture": "amd64",
  "schemaVersion": 1,
$ curl -s -H 'Accept: application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json' https://quay.io/v2/freeipa/freeipa-server/manifests/fedora-43 | jq | head -5
{
  "schemaVersion": 2,
  "mediaType": "application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json",
  "config": {
    "mediaType": "application/vnd.oci.image.config.v1+json",
```
or
```
$ curl -s -H 'Accept: application/vnd.oci.image.index.v1+json' https://quay.io/v2/freeipa/freeipa-server/manifests/rocky-9-4.11.0 | jq | head -5
{
  "tag": "rocky-9-4.11.0",
  "name": "freeipa/freeipa-server",
  "architecture": "amd64",
  "schemaVersion": 1,
$ curl -s -H 'Accept: application/vnd.oci.image.index.v1+json' https://quay.io/v2/freeipa/freeipa-server/manifests/rocky-9 | jq | head -6
{
  "schemaVersion": 2,
  "mediaType": "application/vnd.oci.image.index.v1+json",
  "manifests": [
    {
      "mediaType": "application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json",
```
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
